input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "SURVEY" --files "llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py" --note "Adding get_image_mimetype() method to ImageBlock class; will refactor image_to_base64() model_validator to use it instead of inline filetype.guess() logic"

coop-send agent1 --type "DRAFT" --files "llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py" --region "normalize_image_bytes helper + ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype + ImageBlock.image_to_base64" --code "def normalize_image_bytes(image_bytes: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    \"\"\"Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of ``(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`` where
    ``encoded_image`` is the base64-encoded representation to store on the
    model, and ``decoded_for_guessing`` is the raw (decoded) bytes suitable
    for MIME type detection via ``filetype.guess``.
    \"\"\"
    try:
        decoded = base64.b64decode(image_bytes, validate=True)
        if base64.b64encode(decoded) == image_bytes:
            return image_bytes, decoded
    except Exception:
        pass

    return base64.b64encode(image_bytes), image_bytes


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal[\"image\"] = \"image\"
    image: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator(\"url\", mode=\"after\")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
        \"\"\"Store the url as Anyurl.\"\"\"
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        \"\"\"Lazily resolve and cache the image mimetype.

        Computes the mimetype only when it hasn't been resolved yet, caching
        the result on the instance for future accesses.
        \"\"\"
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype

        if not self.image:
            return None

        _, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode=\"after\")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        \"\"\"Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        \"\"\"
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, _ = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype()

        return self" --note "Adjusted: my task spec requires get_image_mimetype() to be a no-arg method usable standalone by any external caller (not just internal helper needing pre-decoded bytes) -- it must check cache, guess+cache if unset, and return. So I moved the normalize_image_bytes call inside get_image_mimetype for guessing, and image_to_base64 now just normalizes self.image then calls self.get_image_mimetype() (accepting the minor redundant re-decode). Let me know if this works for you or propose changes."

    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of ``(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`` where
    ``encoded_image`` is the base64-encoded representation to store on the
    model, and ``decoded_for_guessing`` is the raw (decoded) bytes suitable

    except Exception:
        pass

class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
    image: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    def get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_for_guessing: bytes) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the image mimetype.

        Guesses the mimetype using the filetype library only when it has not
        already been set, caching the result on the instance for future calls.
        """
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype

        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)

        self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)

        return self

        Guesses the mimetype using the filetype library only when it has not

When image_to_base64 calls it, it passes the already-normalized bytes (your optimization, no double-decode). When any OTHER external caller calls get_image_mimetype() with no args, it self-derives via normalize_image_bytes(self.image) -- this is required by my task spec so the method is standalone/reusable, not just an internal helper. image_to_base64 body stays exactly as in your last message (self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)). normalize_image_bytes function unchanged from your last message. Please confirm."

content.content:
[
  {
    "from": "agent1",
    "to": "agent2",
    "content": "type: AGREE
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: normalize_image_bytes helper + ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype + ImageBlock.image_to_base64
code: def normalize_image_bytes(image_bytes: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where
    encoded_image is the base64-encoded representation to store on the
    model, and decoded_for_guessing is the raw (decoded) bytes suitable
    for MIME type detection via filetype.guess.
    """
    try:
        decoded = base64.b64decode(image_bytes, validate=True)
        if base64.b64encode(decoded) == image_bytes:
            return image_bytes, decoded
    except Exception:
        pass

    return base64.b64encode(image_bytes), image_bytes


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
    image: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the image mimetype.

        Computes the mimetype only when it hasn't been resolved yet, caching
        the result on the instance for future accesses.
        """
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype

        if not self.image:
            return None

        _, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import base64
4	from enum import Enum
5	from io import BytesIO
6	from typing import (
7	    Annotated,
8	    Any,
9	    AsyncGenerator,
10	    Generator,
11	    List,
12	    Literal,
13	    Optional,
14	    Union,
15	)
16	
17	import filetype
18	import requests
19	from typing_extensions import Self
20	
21	from llama_index.core.bridge.pydantic import (
22	    AnyUrl,
23	    BaseModel,
24	    ConfigDict,
25	    Field,
26	    FilePath,
27	    field_serializer,
28	    field_validator,
29	    model_validator,
30	)
31	from llama_index.core.constants import DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW, DEFAULT_NUM_OUTPUTS
32	
33	
34	class MessageRole(str, Enum):
35	    """Message role."""
36	
37	    SYSTEM = "system"
38	    USER = "user"
39	    ASSISTANT = "assistant"
40	    FUNCTION = "function"
41	    TOOL = "tool"
42	    CHATBOT = "chatbot"
43	    MODEL = "model"
44	
45	
46	class TextBlock(BaseModel):
47	    block_type: Literal["text"] = "text"
48	    text: str
49	
50	
51	class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
52	    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
53	    image: bytes | None = None
54	    path: FilePath | None = None
55	    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
56	    image_mimetype: str | None = None
57	    detail: str | None = None
58	
59	    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
60	    @classmethod
61	    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
62	        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
63	        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
64	            return url
65	        return AnyUrl(url=url)
66	
67	    @model_validator(mode="after")
68	    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
69	        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
70	
71	        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
72	        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
73	        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
74	        """
75	        if not self.image:
76	            return self
77	
78	        if not self.image_mimetype:
79	            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
80	            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
81	
82	        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
83	
84	        return self
85	
86	    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
87	        """Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.
88	
89	        Args:
90	            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
91	        """
92	        if self.image is not None:
93	            if as_base64:
94	                return BytesIO(self.image)
95	            return BytesIO(base64.b64decode(self.image))
96	        elif self.path is not None:
97	            img_bytes = self.path.read_bytes()
98	            if as_base64:
99	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
100	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
101	        elif self.url is not None:
102	            # load image from URL
103	            response = requests.get(str(self.url))
104	            img_bytes = response.content
105	            if as_base64:
106	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
107	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
108	        else:
109	            raise ValueError("No image found in the chat message!")
110	
111	
112	ContentBlock = Annotated[
113	    Union[TextBlock, ImageBlock], Field(discriminator="block_type")
114	]

input.command:
python -c "
from llama_index.core.base.llms.types import ImageBlock
import base64

# Test with raw bytes (PNG header)
png_bytes = b'\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n' + b'0'*20
block = ImageBlock(image=png_bytes)
print('mimetype:', block.get_image_mimetype())
print('image is base64:', block.image)
print('cached mimetype:', block.image_mimetype)

# Test with explicit mimetype set
block2 = ImageBlock(image=png_bytes, image_mimetype='image/custom')
print('explicit mimetype preserved:', block2.get_image_mimetype())

# Test with no image
block3 = ImageBlock()
print('no image mimetype:', block3.get_image_mimetype())
"

diff --git a/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py b/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
index 00e30cf23..a1755e366 100644
--- a/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
+++ b/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
@@ -48,6 +48,24 @@ class TextBlock(BaseModel):
     text: str
 
 
+def normalize_image_bytes(image_bytes: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
+    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.
+
+    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where
+    encoded_image is the base64-encoded representation to store on the
+    model, and decoded_for_guessing is the raw (decoded) bytes suitable
+    for MIME type detection via filetype.guess.
+    """
+    try:
+        decoded = base64.b64decode(image_bytes, validate=True)
+        if base64.b64encode(decoded) == image_bytes:
+            return image_bytes, decoded
+    except Exception:
+        pass
+
+    return base64.b64encode(image_bytes), image_bytes
+
+
 class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
     block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
     image: bytes | None = None
@@ -64,6 +82,23 @@ class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
             return url
         return AnyUrl(url=url)
 
+    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
+        """Lazily resolve and cache the image mimetype.
+
+        Computes the mimetype only when it hasn't been resolved yet, caching
+        the result on the instance for future accesses.
+        """
+        if self.image_mimetype:
+            return self.image_mimetype
+
+        if not self.image:
+            return None
+
+        _, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
+        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
+        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
+        return self.image_mimetype
+
     @model_validator(mode="after")
     def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
         """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
@@ -75,11 +110,8 @@ class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
         if not self.image:
             return self
 
-        if not self.image_mimetype:
-            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
-            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
-
-        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
+        self.image, _ = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
+        self.get_image_mimetype()
 
         return self

content.text:
The patch is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. Summary:

- Added a `get_image_mimetype()` method to `ImageBlock` that lazily resolves and caches the MIME type (my assigned feature).
- Coordinated with agent1 (also touching `image_to_base64`) via the SURVEY/DRAFT/AGREE protocol; we converged on a shared implementation that also includes their `normalize_image_bytes()` helper (detects pre-encoded base64 input), since both features modify the same `image_to_base64` validator.
- `image_to_base64` now delegates mimetype resolution to `get_image_mimetype()` instead of inline `filetype.guess()` logic.
- Verified behavior manually (fresh image, pre-set mimetype, no image) and ran `tests/base/llms/test_types.py` — all 12 tests pass.

- Added a `get_image_mimetype()` method to `ImageBlock` that lazily resolves and caches the MIME type (my assigned feature).
- Coordinated with agent1 (also touching `image_to_base64`) via the SURVEY/DRAFT/AGREE protocol; we converged on a shared implementation that also includes their `normalize_image_bytes()` helper (detects pre-encoded base64 input), since both features modify the same `image_to_base64` validator.
- `image_to_base64` now delegates mimetype resolution to `get_image_mimetype()` instead of inline `filetype.guess()` logic.
- Verified behavior manually (fresh image, pre-set mimetype, no image) and ran `tests/base/llms/test_types.py` — all 12 tests pass.
